ingredients.name: L-Cysteine·HCl
ingredients.rationale: Cysteine as reducing agent (gentler than Na2S for fermenters; supplies sulfur amino acid).

ingredients.name: Glucose
ingredients.rationale: Carbon source from RecipeContext: gapseq: UDP-α-D-glucose biosynthesis (100%)

ingredients.name: Lactose
ingredients.rationale: Carbon source from RecipeContext: gapseq: D-galactose detoxification (100%)

ingredients.name: Yeast extract
ingredients.rationale: Yeast extract supplies amino acids, B-vitamins, and growth factors that fermenters typically can't biosynthesize.

ingredients.name: Phosphate buffer (KH2PO4 + K2HPO4)
ingredients.rationale: 30 mM phosphate buffer adjusted to pH 7.0

ingredients.name: NaCl
ingredients.rationale: Standard salinity baseline (5 g/L) for non-halophile

ingredients.name: SL-10 trace metal solution
ingredients.rationale: SL-10 (Tschech & Pfennig 1984): Fe, Zn, Mn, B, Co, Cu, Ni, Mo at standard concentrations. 1 mL/L final.

ingredients.name: Wolin's vitamin solution
ingredients.rationale: Wolin's vitamins (Wolin et al. 1963): biotin, folic acid, pyridoxine, thiamine, riboflavin, nicotinic acid, pantothenate, B12, p-aminobenzoic acid, lipoic acid. 1 mL/L final.

gas_phase.rationale: N2 anaerobic headspace; no CO2 needed.

composition_rationale: Fermentation: substrate-level phosphorylation. Recipe provides fermentable carbon source under anaerobic conditions; no terminal electron acceptor needed.
uncertainty_flags: Scalindua-clade detection failure is a MAG completeness limitation: predicted proteome lacks hzsA / hdh, so anammox could not be detected. The composed recipe is for the highest-confidence DETECTED mode but does NOT reflect the organism's true biology. Treat as ESCALATE_STRUCTURAL.
escalation_reason: Scalindua MAG lacks hzsA/hdh in predicted proteome. The composed recipe routes to the next-best detected mode but is not biologically correct. Manual annotation or improved MAG required.
